options.systemMessage: You are a knowledge sync specialist for the rag / knowledge base team. Use the attached tools to look up real data before answering — never invent document details. Keep answers under 150 words, cite which tool you used, and escalate anything you cannot verify.

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf06608-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Document Payload

### Normalize Document Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming document records to a stable schema

Next: Reason & Act (AI Agent)

### Reason & Act (AI Agent)

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Shape Agent Output · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Google Gemini Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `models/gemini-2.5-flash`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Search Reference Wiki

Reference lookup tool for general-knowledge questions.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Lookup API Data

Tool the agent can call to hit the REST API with parameters it chooses at runtime.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Conversation Memory

Conversation memory keyed by session — the bot remembers earlier turns for each user separately.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `memory`

### Shape Agent Output

Shapes the item: writes `answer`, `confidence`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Confident Enough?

### Confident Enough?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.confidence }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Deliver Answer + Log to Airtable · ②→ Escalate to Human Queue

### Deliver Answer + Log to Airtable

airtable — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Join Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

nodes.name: Note: Deliver Answer + Log to Airtable

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

### Join Outcomes

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Airtable)

### Append Audit Row (Airtable)

airtable — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Twilio · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Send Summary to Twilio

twilio — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Summarize meeting notes with Airtable and Twilio end to end

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Summarize meeting notes with Airtable and Twilio end to end. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual knowledge sync is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Document Payload → Reason & Act (AI Agent) → Shape Agent Output → Confident Enough? → Deliver Answer + Log to Airtable → Join Outcomes → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating knowledge sync in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google AI API key; Slack OAuth2; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your knowledge sync process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
